skill.name: writing-skills
skill.description: Guides creation, editing, and pressure testing of AI skills using test-driven documentation practices.
skill.summary: Create and verify AI skills with test-driven documentation workflows.
skill.author: obra

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium alerts, but review found most are Markdown examples, documentation URLs, hash-like image parameters, or test scenarios. The confirmed risk is a legitimate helper script that invokes Graphviz and writes diagram files. No evidence found for prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Executable Graphviz Renderer Invokes External Process
medium_findings.description: render-graphs.js imports child_process and runs the fixed dot -Tsvg command on Graphviz content extracted from a target SKILL.md. Command injection is not evident because the shell command is fixed and graph content is passed on stdin, but the skill includes executable local tooling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external process calls are directly present in executable JavaScript. The command is fixed and appears legitimate, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Renderer Writes Files Under User-Specified Skill Directory
medium_findings.description: The renderer resolves a user-supplied skill directory, reads SKILL.md, creates a diagrams directory, and writes SVG or DOT files. This is expected behavior for the tool, but it modifies the filesystem wherever the operator points it.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file reads, directory creation, and writes are explicit in the script. The behavior is scoped to the chosen directory but still warrants a publication warning.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Triggered External Command Alerts
low_findings.description: Many external command alerts are commands shown in Markdown examples or test fixtures. They are instructional text and are not executed by the skill during normal loading.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited commands are inside Markdown code fences or examples. They describe usage and do not create an automatic execution path.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Are Static References
low_findings.description: Network alerts point to Anthropic documentation links and hosted images inside a copied best-practices document. No runtime HTTP client or automatic network request was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are embedded in documentation and image markup. I did not find code that fetches them or sends local data to them.

low_findings.title: Home Directory References Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Hidden home directory alerts refer to Claude and Codex skill directory examples. They teach where personal skills live and do not access those paths automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths appear in explanatory Markdown and scenario text. There is no evidence that the skill reads hidden home files on load.

low_findings.title: Blocker Keyword and Weak Crypto Alerts Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The high blocker alerts map to prose, documentation image parameters, file naming examples, and research text. I did not find cryptographic operations, command-and-control behavior, or malicious control flow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are static documentation, examples, or URL parameters rather than executable cryptography or network control logic. The scanner terms match text context, not malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Fixed Shell Command for Graphviz Rendering
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper script runs dot -Tsvg through execSync. The command string is fixed, but it still depends on local Graphviz execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code uses execSync directly with a fixed command. This confirms external command execution without showing command injection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Output Generation
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper creates a diagrams directory and writes generated SVG or DOT outputs in the selected skill directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write operations are explicit and are part of the intended renderer behavior. The risk depends on where the user points the script.

content.value_statement: AI skills can fail when instructions are vague or untested. This skill gives authors a test-driven process for writing, pressure testing, and refining reusable skill documentation.
actual_capabilities: Defines a test-driven workflow for creating and editing skills.
Explains how to write focused skill frontmatter for discovery.
Provides pressure testing methods for discipline-enforcing skills.
Includes reference material on Anthropic skill authoring practices.
Documents patterns for flowcharts, examples, and supporting files.
Includes a helper script for rendering Graphviz diagrams to SVG.
limitations: Requires human judgment to decide whether a skill needs testing.
Does not automatically validate marketplace manifests or package metadata.
The diagram renderer requires Graphviz to be installed locally.
Some examples target Claude skill directories and need adaptation for other agents.

use_cases.title: Write a New Skill
use_cases.description: Create a skill with clear triggers, concise guidance, and supporting references only when needed.

use_cases.title: Pressure Test Skill Instructions
use_cases.description: Design scenarios that reveal when an agent ignores or rationalizes away important guidance.

use_cases.title: Refine Existing Skill Documentation
use_cases.description: Identify vague instructions, close loopholes, and improve discovery metadata before deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to draft a new AI skill for [task]. Include the frontmatter, overview, when to use it, and common mistakes.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Skill Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill description for discovery quality. Make it start with Use when, remove process summary, and add better trigger terms.

prompt_templates.title: Design Pressure Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three pressure scenarios for this skill. Each scenario should combine time pressure, sunk cost, authority, or exhaustion.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor After Failed Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Given these failed pressure test transcripts, identify rationalizations and revise the skill to close each loophole without adding unnecessary length.

output_examples.input: A user wants a skill that enforces test-first bug fixes.
output_examples.output: A concise skill outline with trigger-focused frontmatter, a test-first rule, pressure scenarios, and a rationalization table.

output_examples.input: A maintainer has a long skill that agents often skip.
output_examples.output: A shorter structure with clearer discovery wording, fewer examples, and heavy reference material moved to separate files.

output_examples.input: A reviewer needs to verify a discipline skill before release.
output_examples.output: Three realistic pressure scenarios and a pass criteria checklist for agent compliance under stress.

best_practices: Run a failing baseline scenario before writing or changing a discipline skill.
Keep the description focused on when to use the skill, not the workflow.
Move heavy references and reusable tools into separate files when they improve clarity.
anti_patterns: Do not write narrative session reports as reusable skill guidance.
Do not summarize the whole workflow in the frontmatter description.
Do not deploy a discipline skill without pressure testing it first.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps authors create, edit, and verify AI skills using test-driven documentation practices.

faq.question: Does it create skills automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a structured process, examples, and review criteria for human-guided authoring.

faq.question: Why does it mention TDD?
faq.answer: It adapts test-driven development to documentation by testing agent behavior before and after skill changes.

faq.question: Can it be used with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The concepts apply to Codex, Claude, and Claude Code, with directory paths adjusted as needed.

faq.question: Why is Graphviz included?
faq.answer: The skill uses flowcharts for non-obvious decisions and includes a renderer for SVG diagrams.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is suitable for publication with a warning that the included renderer executes Graphviz and writes files.
